ideal_must_detect.matched_text: not yet disclosed to the market

must_detect.matched_text:
Lakanbay Holdings, Inc. — Internal Legal & Compliance Memo re: Project Sibol (Strictly Confidential; MNPI/PII; Philippines).
Proposed primary share placement on the PSE remains non-public; preliminary commercial terms include Php12.40–13.10 per share, a 180-day lock-up on principal shareholders, and a 15% over-allotment option with Sampaguita Securities, Inc. as sole bookrunner.
Use-of-proceeds includes microgrid solar retrofits and resilience upgrades; per SEC PH Sustainability Reporting Guidelines, draft KPI covenants and external assurance terms are for deal team only and not to be circulated.
Our subsidiary Lakanbay Digital Wallet Corp. (VASP registration pending with BSP) is preparing the closed beta of the PisoLink token rewards program; this is MNPI given potential revenue impact and is covered by the current blackout window.
Incident Response notes that on 27 May, a credential-stuffing attack against the wallet staging environment exposed hashed test emails; no customer funds moved; details are not yet disclosed to the market and must be escalated to BSP and the SEC only through approved channels.
Information barriers: Maintain the Project Sibol clean room; no forwarding of HR payroll files or BIR TIN datasets to the deal dataroom; only the “restricted” list may access paying-agent drafts from BayanSure Bank, Inc.
Insider list and blackout: The Insider List (SRC Rule 12.1) currently includes Atty. Lucia Benitez (Chief Legal), Ms. Pilar Ong (CFO), and Mr. Ramon Villaceran (IT Security Lead); all insiders remain on trading blackout until two full trading days after any Form 17-C disclosure.
Selective disclosure/tipping: Do not share pricing talk or ESG covenant terms with prospective cornerstone investors unless wall-crossed under NDA; family members and suppliers are expressly out of scope.
Educational reuse (benign): The crypto wallet tutorial used in staff onboarding references “wallets,” “stable tokens,” and “gas fees” purely for training and does not access live keys or include MNPI.
Marketing reuse (benign): The ESG microsite draft uses generic illustrations of “carbon credits” and “climate risk” and contains no issuer-specific targets, forecasts, or commercial terms.
HR/tax PII handling: Employee name lists, work emails, and payroll bank account placeholders in the SEC Form 17-A annexes are personal data under the Data Privacy Act and must be pseudonymised before sharing with underwriters not yet cleared for the wall.
Banking ops: The draft trust and paying-agent agreement with BayanSure Bank, Inc. sets a provisional 25 bps fee grid and early redemption mechanics; these commercial terms are MNPI until finalised.
Governance: Any outreach to research analysts must route through Compliance to preserve information barriers; no participation in non-deal roadshows until the trading window reopens.
Filing triggers: Prepare but do not file a Form 17-C; counsel to assess materiality thresholds under the SRC given the cyber event and VASP launch timing; if thresholds are met, coordinate immediate disclosure to the PSE and the SEC.
Contact: Direct questions to Atty. Lucia Benitez at lucia.benitez@lakanbay-holdings.example or Compliance at compliance@lakanbay-holdings.example; hard copies are restricted to Board-only packets.
